title: phlox speciosa subsp. woodhousei
intro: Phlox speciosa subsp. woodhousei, also known as the Wood's phlox or Wood's fragrant phlox, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) that is native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Phlox speciosa subsp. woodhousei include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, or added to salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
